articles.title: Infinite Campus data breach affects 137,000 school staff accounts
articles.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from more than 137,000 school staff accounts in a Salesforce data theft attack that targeted the widely used Infinite Campus K-12 student information system in March. [...]

articles.title: Webinar: How behavioral AI stops phishing and account takeovers
articles.summary: Modern phishing, BEC, and account takeover attacks increasingly bypass traditional email defenses and create operational strain for security teams. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help automate detection, investigation, and remediation to reduce alert fatigue and accelerate response time

articles.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
articles.summary: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-20262 Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Directory or Path Traversal Vulnerability CVE-2026-54420 LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following

articles.title: Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges
articles.summary: Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko admitted to working on the development of a loader for the Conti gang. The post Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems
articles.summary: The pharmaceutical giant says the attackers gained access to personal data stored on the compromised systems. The post Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker
articles.summary: French officials say roughly 73,000 government accounts were affected, while the threat actor claims to have stolen messages and user data from the sovereign Tchap platform. The post French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack
articles.summary: The extortion group threatens to leak 297 GB of data allegedly stolen from the Council of Europe, including employee personal information. The post ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
articles.summary: An attacker tampered with trusted JavaScript files used by WordPress sites running PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse, turning those files into a way to break into the sites. When a site administrator was logged in as the file loaded, the code created an admin account under the attacker's cont

articles.title: Maine Disables Data Breach Portal Due to Fake Submissions
articles.summary: Someone posted fake VRChat and Discord data breach reports on the system, prompting the Maine AG to take action. The post Maine Disables Data Breach Portal Due to Fake Submissions appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Evil MSI Background: BASE64 Statistical Analysis, (Mon, Jun 15th)
articles.summary: I like it when a fellow handler posts a diary entry about images with malicious content. Last one is Xavier: "The Evil MSI Background is Back!".

articles.title: Palo Alto Warns of Active Exploitation of PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN Flaw
articles.summary: Palo Alto Networks has revealed that it has observed "active exploitation" of a recently disclosed PAN-OS vulnerability by an unknown threat actor to obtain unauthorized access to GlobalProtect portals. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), an authentication bypass flaw a

articles.title: Belarus-linked hackers target Gmail accounts of Polish public figures and their families
articles.summary: Poland has warned that Ghostwriter, the Belarus-linked hacker group, has expanded its phishing operations to target personal Gmail accounts belonging to senior public figures and their relatives.

articles.title: Ex-school district employee jailed for hacks on former employer
articles.summary: A former IT employee at an Iowa school district was sentenced to 21 months in prison after conducting a prolonged cyberattack against the former employer that disrupted classroom operations, deleted accounts, and caused tens of thousands of dollars in damages. [...]

articles.title: NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks
articles.summary: By default, npm install will no longer execute scripts from dependencies, unless explicitly allowed. The post NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
articles.summary: Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20253, is rated 9.8 on the CVSS scoring system. "In Splunk Enterpris

articles.title: Maine disables data breach notification portal after fake disclosures
articles.summary: Maine has taken its public data breach reporting portal offline after fraudulent breach disclosures were published on the state's website, prompting a review of procedures to prevent abuse in the future. [...]

articles.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
articles.summary: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them. The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF ro

articles.title: 400+ Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Install Rust Credential Stealer
articles.summary: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them. The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF ro

articles.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
articles.summary: Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Americans. The network is said to be behind the development and management of a phishing-as-a-service (Ph

articles.title: phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
articles.summary: A 10-year-old authentication bypass vulnerability discovered in the phpBB forum software allows an attacker to log in as any user, including administrators. [...]

articles.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
articles.summary: Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself. Sygnia, which tracks the group as Velvet Ant, says it backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components that decide who is allowed to sign in, p

articles.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
articles.summary: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. [...]

articles.title: Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims
articles.summary: About 7 million customers of the genetics testing company had their data stolen by hackers starting in April 2023, and many had their information posted on the dark web.

articles.title: Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer
articles.summary: More than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) are distributing a Linux rootkit and infostealer malware targeting credentials and access tokens. [...]

articles.title: In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million Coupang Fine
articles.summary: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: ICS device exposure remains flat as attack surface widens, Microsoft issues incident response playbook for AI, IBM and AT&T accused of hack cover-ups. The post In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million

articles.title: South Korea hits Coupang with record $409 million fine over data breach
articles.summary: The penalty is the largest ever issued by the commission for a personal data breach, surpassing the record 134.8 billion won ($88.8 million) fine levied against SK Telecom earlier this year.

articles.title: Early Warning Signs of Supply-Chain Attacks Live in the Dark Web
articles.summary: GitHub access sales, leaked repositories, and stolen API keys can all become supply-chain attack footholds. Flare explores how underground forums expose early signals tied to software supply-chain risk. [...]

articles.title: Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have described what they say is a new class of attack that can trick artificial intelligence (AI) coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines. Called Agentjacking by Tenet Security, the attack can be triggered by means of a fake error report crafted usin

articles.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
articles.summary: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-35273 Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools Missing Authentication for Critical Function Vulnerability This type of vulnerability is a frequent attack vect

articles.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack
articles.summary: The hackers published 5GB of data, including customer personal information and credentials for the RTKBase platform. The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Rethinking MDR as Attackers and Defenders Embrace AI
articles.summary: For most of the past decade, managed detection and response was the answer to a real problem. Security teams couldn't staff around the clock, couldn't hire enough analysts, and needed someone else to handle the alert queue. MDR stepped in. It worked well enough. Until now. The threat landscape has c

articles.title: Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical trials data
articles.summary: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk, the world's largest producer of insulin, disclosed a data breach affecting patient information from some clinical trials. [...]

articles.title: LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of three now-patched security flaws impacting LangGraph, including a critical vulnerability chain that could result in remote code execution. LangGraph is an open-source framework created by LangChain to build complex, stateful, and multi-agent artifi

articles.title: Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots
articles.summary: The critical-severity OS command injection vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges. The post Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The browser refresh resolved critical and high-severity security defects, including a dozen use-after-free bugs. The post Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Anthropic Disputes Fable 5 AI Jailbreak
articles.summary: An AI hacker claims to have achieved a prompt-based jailbreak shortly after Fable 5’s launch, but Anthropic says it’s not a real jailbreak. The post Anthropic Disputes Fable 5 AI Jailbreak appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti flaw by Sunday
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered government agencies to patch an actively exploited Ivanti Sentry flaw within three days, as mandated by the newly issued Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 26-04. [...]

articles.title: Over 73,000 French govt employees affected in Tchap messenger breach
articles.summary: The French government revealed that a recent breach of its Tchap encrypted messaging platform affects the accounts of over 73,000 employees in the French public sector. [...]

articles.title: Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters
articles.summary: Oracle has mitigated CVE-2026-35273, but it has not publicly confirmed the vulnerability’s in-the-wild exploitation. The post Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Europol Disrupts AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
articles.summary: Authorities in Europe have disrupted AudiA6, a cryptocurrency laundering service used by ransomware gangs and cybercriminal networks. Europol, in a statement issued Thursday, said the dismantling of AudiA6 cut off a "key financial pipeline used to wash hundreds of millions in illicit profits." The s

articles.title: Maine breach portal abused to publish fake data breach disclosures
articles.summary: In an unusual misinformation campaign, fraudulent data breach disclosures were submitted to Maine's official breach portal and publicly posted before their legitimacy could be verified, prompting companies to deny the claims. [...]

articles.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
articles.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion crew exploited an unpatched flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft to break into enterprise systems, steal data, and demand payment to keep it private. The campaign hit universities hardest. Google's Mandiant attributes it to the group it tracks as UNC6240, and dates the activity betwe

articles.title: Oracle mitigates PeopleSoft zero-day exploited in data theft attacks
articles.summary: Oracle is warning about a critical PeopleSoft Suite zero-day vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-35273 that allows unauthenticated remote code execution, with the flaw actively exploited in ShinyHunter data theft attacks. [...]

articles.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets
articles.summary: Two security teams have shown, in separate research published this week, that OpenClaw, the popular self-hosted AI agent, can be driven to run attacker-controlled code or hand over sensitive data through ordinary-looking inputs. Imperva buried instructions inside shared contacts, vCards, and locatio

articles.title: New GreatXML Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker via Recovery Partition XML Files
articles.summary: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse and MSNightmare) has released a new Windows BitLocker bypass dubbed GreatXML, a day after they published an exploit for Microsoft Defender. "This was an accidental discovery, it took a total of 4 hours to find this," the researcher said in a

articles.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Claims 478 Victims, Can Spread Like a Worm
articles.summary: A new analysis of The Gentlemen operation has revealed that the financially motivated threat group initially operated as an affiliate responsible for conducting double extortion attacks, while leveraging resources from various ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) schemes like LockBit (aka Tenacious Mantis

articles.title: Authorities dismantle 'AudiA6' ransomware crypto-laundering service
articles.summary: Law enforcement has dismantled the “AudiA6” cryptocurrency service allegedly used by ransomware actors and other cybercriminals to launder more than $380 million. [...]

articles.title: British high school sends students home following cyberattack
articles.summary: Great Marlow School, which has 1,428 pupils according to the Department for Education (DfE), said it was set to remain closed while it works with specialist IT and cybersecurity professionals to resolve the issue.

articles.title: Hacker linked to Void Blizzard faces charges over cyberespionage campaign
articles.summary: Denis Obrezko, 36, made his initial appearance in federal court in Boston on Tuesday after being transferred to U.S. custody from Thailand, where he was arrested last November.

articles.title: Why AI-driven threats are exposing the limits of MSP security stacks
articles.summary: AI-driven attacks are exposing the limits of fragmented MSP security stacks and slow response workflows. Kaseya breaks down why integrated security, automation, and recovery are becoming essential. [...]
